### Fix 2
Lines: 72, 85
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: span

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

In PatternFly v6, icon content in <HelperTextItem> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

  After:  <HelperTextItem variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <HelperTextItem> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:

  After:  <HelperTextItem icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</HelperTextItem>

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/odf/components/s3-browser/create-or-edit-cors-rules/CorsFormContents.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand the current code.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1** (Lines 73, 86): `Button.variant` type changed — but the values used are `ButtonVariant.plain` and `ButtonVariant.link`, which are both still valid in the new type. The type just added `'stateful'`. No code change needed — this is already compatible.

**Fix 2** (Lines 72, 85): Icon children in `<Button>` must be moved to the `icon` prop.

- Line 72-82: `<Button variant={ButtonVariant.plain} ...><TrashIcon /></Button>` — This Button contains ONLY an icon (`TrashIcon`). Move it to the `icon` prop and make self-closing.
- Line 85-96: `<Button variant={ButtonVariant.link} ...><PlusCircleIcon /> {addButtonText}</Button>` — This Button has an icon AND text. Move just the icon to the `icon` prop.

**Fix 3** (Line 154): `<HelperTextItem variant={ValidatedOptions.error}>` — This contains only text children (error messages), no icon. Looking at the fix description, it says to move icon content to the `icon` prop. But there's no icon in this HelperTextItem — it contains only text. The rule may have fired because HelperTextItem's API changed, but there's no icon to move here. No change needed for this one.

Let me apply Fix 2:

arguments.after:
<Button
            variant={ButtonVariant.plain}
            onClick={() =>
              dispatch({
                type: actionType,
                payload: actionState.filter((_, i) => i !== index),
              })
            }
            icon={<TrashIcon />}
          />
arguments.before:
<Button
            variant={ButtonVariant.plain}
            onClick={() =>
              dispatch({
                type: actionType,
                payload: actionState.filter((_, i) => i !== index),
              })
            }
          >
            <TrashIcon />
          </Button>

arguments.after:
<Button
        variant={ButtonVariant.link}
        onClick={() =>
          dispatch({
            type: actionType,
            payload: [...actionState, ''],
          })
        }
        className="s3-cors-button--margin"
        icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}
      >
        {addButtonText}
      </Button>
arguments.before:
<Button
        variant={ButtonVariant.link}
        onClick={() =>
          dispatch({
            type: actionType,
            payload: [...actionState, ''],
          })
        }
        className="s3-cors-button--margin"
      >
        <PlusCircleIcon /> {addButtonText}
      </Button>

content.text: Now let me verify the final file looks correct:

The file looks correct. All changes have been applied properly. Let me verify the imports are still clean — both `TrashIcon` and `PlusCircleIcon` are still used (in the `icon` props), so the imports remain valid.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant` type changed (lines 73, 86)
**No change needed.** The values used (`ButtonVariant.plain` and `ButtonVariant.link`) are both present in both the old and new type union. The type change only *added* `'stateful'` as a new option — no existing usage is affected.

### Fix 2 — Icon children to `icon` prop on `<Button>` (lines 72, 85)
**Applied.** Two changes made:
1. **Line 72–82 (trash button):** Moved `<TrashIcon />` from children to the `icon` prop and made the `<Button>` self-closing, since it contained only the icon.
   - Before: `<Button variant={ButtonVariant.plain} ...><TrashIcon /></Button>`
   - After: `<Button variant={ButtonVariant.plain} ... icon={<TrashIcon />} />`
2. **Line 85–96 (add button):** Moved `<PlusCircleIcon />` from children to the `icon` prop, keeping `{addButtonText}` as the remaining child content.
   - Before: `<Button variant={ButtonVariant.link} ...><PlusCircleIcon /> {addButtonText}</Button>`
   - After: `<Button variant={ButtonVariant.link} ... icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>{addButtonText}</Button>`

### Fix 3 — Icon children to `icon` prop on `<HelperTextItem>` (line 154)
**No change needed.** The `<HelperTextItem>` at line 154 contains only text children (error message strings), not icon elements. There is no icon to migrate to the `icon` prop. The rule fired as a false positive on this location.

The file looks correct. All changes have been applied properly. Let me verify the imports are still clean — both `TrashIcon` and `PlusCircleIcon` are still used (in the `icon` props), so the imports remain valid.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant` type changed (lines 73, 86)
**No change needed.** The values used (`ButtonVariant.plain` and `ButtonVariant.link`) are both present in both the old and new type union. The type change only *added* `'stateful'` as a new option — no existing usage is affected.

### Fix 2 — Icon children to `icon` prop on `<Button>` (lines 72, 85)
**Applied.** Two changes made:
1. **Line 72–82 (trash button):** Moved `<TrashIcon />` from children to the `icon` prop and made the `<Button>` self-closing, since it contained only the icon.
   - Before: `<Button variant={ButtonVariant.plain} ...><TrashIcon /></Button>`
   - After: `<Button variant={ButtonVariant.plain} ... icon={<TrashIcon />} />`
2. **Line 85–96 (add button):** Moved `<PlusCircleIcon />` from children to the `icon` prop, keeping `{addButtonText}` as the remaining child content.
   - Before: `<Button variant={ButtonVariant.link} ...><PlusCircleIcon /> {addButtonText}</Button>`
   - After: `<Button variant={ButtonVariant.link} ... icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>{addButtonText}</Button>`

### Fix 3 — Icon children to `icon` prop on `<HelperTextItem>` (line 154)
**No change needed.** The `<HelperTextItem>` at line 154 contains only text children (error message strings), not icon elements. There is no icon to migrate to the `icon` prop. The rule fired as a false positive on this location.
